seoTitle: Scone vs Scotland Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Scone and Scotland Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Scone vs Scotland Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Scone and Scotland Island.
heading: Scone vs Scotland Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Scone, New South Wales and Scotland Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Scone has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Scotland Island (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Scone or Scotland Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Scone has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Scotland Island scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Scone scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Scotland Island scores 42/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Scone or Scotland Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Scone has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.6% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
